sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: FCPI Stock Fund Price and Chart — CBOE:FCPI
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the Fidelity Stocks for Inflation ETF (FCPI), detailing its price, chart, key statistics like AUM and expense ratio, and investment strategy. It explains that FCPI invests in large- and mid-cap US equities designed to perform well in inflationary environments, with a focus on sectors like energy and materials. The article also includes technical analysis, seasonal data, and a frequently asked questions section covering various aspects of the ETF for potential investors.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean to swap ships on Southampton summer 2027 sailings
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean will replace Mariner of the Seas with the larger Freedom of the Seas for its Southampton summer 2027 sailings, citing strong guest resonance with the Freedom Class. This change means Freedom of the Seas' previously scheduled Miami cruises between May and September 2027 have been canceled, with affected guests offered alternative cruises or full refunds. Itinerary details for the new Southampton departures will be announced later.

feed.title: Canvas Wealth Advisors LLC Has $1.99 Million Holdings in ServiceNow, Inc. $NOW
feed.summary: Canvas Wealth Advisors LLC significantly increased its stake in ServiceNow, Inc. by 1,995% in the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to $1.99 million. Other institutional investors like Vanguard Group, State Street Corp, and Nordea Investment Management also adjusted their positions. Despite recent insider selling and a slight dip in stock price, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $192.61, citing strong subscription revenue growth and increasing AI adoption as key positives for ServiceNow.

feed.title: Canvas Wealth Advisors LLC Raises Holdings in The Clorox Company $CLX

Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Clorox, which is currently trading at $104.71 with a market capitalization of $12.66 billion. The company recently reported quarterly earnings that missed analyst estimates and declared a quarterly dividend of $1.24 per share.

feed.title: Oracle (ORCL) Price Target Lowered on Revenue Conversion Concerns
feed.summary: Argus has reduced its price target for Oracle (ORCL) shares to $225 from $384, while maintaining a Buy rating. The adjustment stems from analyst concerns regarding whether Oracle's improved backlog will successfully convert into actual revenue. Despite this, Oracle exceeded Q3 2026 earnings estimates, reporting $17.2 billion in revenue, driven primarily by an 84% year-over-year increase in Infrastructure as a Service revenue.

feed.title: Equinix Inc. stock faces data center demand surge amid AI boom and global expansion push
feed.summary: Equinix Inc. (EQIX) is experiencing a surge in demand for its data center services, driven by the artificial intelligence boom and global expansion efforts. The company reported strong Q4 results with accelerated bookings from AI-driven workloads and hyperscaler investments. Its global footprint and strategic positioning in edge computing contribute to stable growth and a strong outlook for multi-year expansion, making it an attractive investment for US portfolios.

feed.title: Brady Family Wealth LLC Sells 2,321 Shares of Apple Inc. $AAPL
feed.summary: Brady Family Wealth LLC reduced its stake in Apple Inc. by 2.3% in the fourth quarter, selling 2,321 shares and now holding 100,543 shares valued at $27.334 million, making Apple its largest position at 8.4% of its holdings. Despite this sale, investor sentiment remains largely positive, with 67.73% institutional ownership and a "Moderate Buy" consensus from analysts, supported by Apple's initiatives in monetization and AI. However, the company faces near-term challenges from UK regulatory changes and softer iPhone shipments in China.

feed.title: Assenagon Asset Management S.A. Reduces Holdings in Intercontinental Exchange Inc. $ICE

The company recently surpassed quarterly EPS estimates and announced a quarterly dividend, while institutional investors collectively own 89.30% of the stock.

feed.title: Avanza Fonder AB Increases Stock Position in The Home Depot, Inc. $HD
feed.summary: Avanza Fonder AB has increased its stake in The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) by 4.7% during the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to 104,923 shares valued at over $36 million. The home improvement retailer recently announced strong Q4 results, surpassing earnings estimates, and provided positive FY2026 EPS guidance. Analysts generally maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for HD, citing strategic initiatives like the expansion of its Pro distribution network through acquisitions and new store openings as drivers for long-term growth.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive Co stock faces governance scrutiny and dividend boost ahead of 2026 annual meeting
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive Co (ISIN: US1941621039) is facing investor scrutiny over a shareholder proposal advocating for an independent board chair, which management opposes, ahead of its May 8, 2026, annual meeting. Simultaneously, the company announced a quarterly dividend increase to $0.53 per share, effective Q2 2026, marking its 62nd consecutive annual hike. These developments unfold against a backdrop of strong Q4 earnings, where EPS and revenue surpassed estimates, leading analysts to maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $94.75.

feed.title: Industrial Vending Machine Market Is Going to Boom |• Fastenal • Grainger • MSC Industrial Direct
feed.summary: Worldwide Market Reports has published a comprehensive research study on the Industrial Vending Machine Market, forecasting its size and growth from 2026 to 2033. The report analyzes key market drivers, challenges, opportunities, and restraints, utilizing SWOT and Porter's Five Forces analysis to assess the competitive landscape. It segments the market by types and applications, covering major players like Fastenal, Grainger, and MSC Industrial Direct, and provides regional analysis for North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, the Middle East and Africa, and South America.

feed.title: Will Conference Focus on Cardiovascular Platforms and FARAPULSE, WATCHMAN Shape Boston Scientific's (BSX) Narrative?
feed.summary: Boston Scientific recently presented at the Life Science Intelligence Summit, where leaders discussed global business services and peripheral interventions, particularly focusing on cardiovascular growth platforms like FARAPULSE and WATCHMAN. The company's investment narrative centers on its ability to integrate these platforms, supported by a new US$3.0 billion credit agreement, despite challenges in electrophysiology and ongoing concerns about reimbursement risks. Analysts project Boston Scientific to achieve $27.0 billion in revenue and $5.2 billion in earnings by 2029, suggesting a 46% upside to its current price.

feed.title: Realty Income Term Loan Reshapes Debt Mix For Community Power Deal
feed.summary: Realty Income (NYSE:O) has secured a $694 million long-term unsecured term loan from Goldman Sachs, which will be used to finance a municipal prepay transaction for San Diego Community Power. This financing, structured with fixed rates and cross-currency swaps, aims to lower the blended cost of debt to 4.34% and introduces euro exposure, aligning with the company's European expansion strategy. While this move provides flexible funding for long-duration assets, investors are cautioned to monitor existing leverage, interest coverage ratios, and foreign exchange risks, especially given previous concerns about interest payments not being well covered by earnings.

feed.title: A Look At Las Vegas Sands (LVS) Valuation As Recent Share Price Momentum Cools
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) has seen its share price momentum cool recently, despite posting strong revenues and net income. While Simply Wall St's narrative suggests LVS is 18% undervalued with a fair value of $65.85, primarily due to the expected boost from The Londoner in Macao and continued growth from Marina Bay Sands in Singapore, a Discounted Cash Flow model indicates the stock might be overvalued at its current price of $54.17 compared to an estimated future cash flow value of $28.13. Investors are advised to consider both valuation angles and key risks before making an investment decision.

feed.title: Is Vistra (VST) Using PJM’s Co‑Location Rules Fight To Sharpen Its Data Center Strategy?
feed.summary: Vistra Corp. has filed a protest against PJM Interconnection’s co-located facilities plan, arguing that its ambiguities and delays could hinder critical data center and AI infrastructure development. This action aligns with Vistra's strategy to capitalize on growing data center power needs, aiming to convert them into long-term contracted cash flows despite balance sheet pressures from debt-funded growth. The company's stance on these regulations could significantly impact its projected revenue and earnings, especially given potential regulatory risks surrounding its fossil fuel assets.

feed.title: Microchip Technology wins Nimbus Innovation Award for SmartRAID 4300 NVMe storage system at CloudFest 2026
feed.summary: Microchip Technology received the Nimbus Innovation Award for Best Storage System Innovation at CloudFest 2026 for its Adaptec SmartRAID 4300 NVMe RAID storage accelerators. These systems enhance storage performance by optimizing direct CPU to NVMe data paths and offloading parity calculations, effectively overcoming traditional RAID bottlenecks. This award highlights Microchip Technology's ongoing dedication to advancing data storage solutions.

feed.title: Q4 Research Tools & Consumables Earnings: Bio-Techne (NASDAQ:TECH) Earns Top Marks
feed.summary: Bio-Techne reported strong Q4 results, exceeding analyst expectations for revenue, though its stock saw an 18.5% decline post-reporting. The broader research tools & consumables sector had a satisfactory Q4, with revenues beating estimates by 1.2%, but share prices for many companies in the sector declined significantly. The article also reviews Q4 performance for other companies like Revvity, Mettler-Toledo, Sotera Health Company, and Agilent, highlighting varying results and market reactions amidst shifting market anxieties from AI disruption to geopolitical risks.

feed.title: FCSH ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:FCSH
feed.summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of the Federated Hermes Short Duration Corporate ETF (FCSH), detailing its investment strategy, holdings, and financial performance. FCSH primarily invests in investment-grade, USD-denominated corporate bonds, aiming for a dollar-weighted average duration of 1.5 to 3.5 years. The ETF has assets under management of $64.24 million, an expense ratio of 0.30%, and pays monthly dividends with a yield of 4.13%.

feed.title: Fortune Brands Innovations Hits Day Low at $38.38 Amid Price Pressure
feed.summary: Fortune Brands Innovations Inc. (NYSE: FBIN) experienced a significant stock decline, dropping 9.61% to an intraday low of $38.38, contrasting with a minor S&P 500 decrease. The company faces long-term challenges, including a 22.67% year-to-date decline and contracting net sales, while financial metrics show high valuation and low cash reserves. As a small-cap player in the building products industry, it continues to navigate a difficult market.

feed.title: EnerSys to close Mexico plant, shift production to Missouri By Investing.com
feed.summary: EnerSys (NYSE:ENS) announced it will close its lead-acid battery manufacturing facility in Tijuana, Mexico, and move most production to its Thin Plate Pure Lead plant in Springfield, Missouri. This restructuring is expected to incur a pre-tax charge of approximately $37 million but is projected to deliver an annual pre-tax benefit of about $20 million starting in fiscal year 2028. The company's shares have seen an 83% return over the past year, and management is aggressively buying back shares, signaling confidence in its strategic direction.

feed.title: Etsy Inc. stock faces renewed pressure amid e-commerce slowdown and macroeconomic headwinds in Q1 2026
feed.summary: Etsy Inc. is facing significant pressure in early 2026 due to declining gross merchandise sales, increased competition from Amazon and Temu, and a broader e-commerce slowdown caused by macroeconomic factors. The company reported flat GMS for Q1 2026 and a dip in active buyers, prompting analysts to reassess its growth prospects. Etsy is strategizing to combat these challenges through AI-driven personalization, the integration of its Depop acquisition, and upgrades to seller tools, while US investors monitor its performance closely ahead of upcoming Q1 earnings.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser Giving Fund invests $13,500 in Coos County nonprofits

The company is currently accepting applications for its 2026 grant cycle.

feed.title: Coca‑Cola system generated $3b for South Africa in 2024 – Study
feed.summary: A new independent study has revealed that the Coca-Cola system generated R51.2 billion (approximately $3 billion) in value-added economic activity in South Africa in 2024, supporting over 87,000 jobs. The study, conducted by Steward Redqueen, highlights Coca-Cola's deep integration into the local economy through local hiring, production, distribution, and sourcing. It also noted the company's participation in a nearly $25 million Africa Water Stewardship Initiative.

feed.title: Raja Sengupta joins Verisk as President of Life Solutions
feed.summary: Verisk, a global data analytics and technology provider, has appointed Raja Sengupta as President of its Life Solutions business. Sengupta will lead the development and delivery of data analytics, software, and AI-powered solutions for life and annuity insurers. He brings over two decades of experience scaling global software businesses and a strong track record of embedding AI solutions into client workflows.

feed.title: Korea's Alteogen signs $549 M deal with Biogen to develop Hybrozyme™-based subcutaneous biologics
feed.summary: South Korea's Alteogen Inc. has entered into an exclusive licensing agreement with US-based Biogen Inc. for the development and commercialization of subcutaneous (SC) formulations of biologics using Alteogen's Hybrozyme™ technology. Alteogen will receive an upfront payment of $20 million and is eligible for up to an additional $549 million in development, regulatory, and sales milestone payments, plus royalties on net sales. This deal grants Biogen rights to develop SC formulations for two biologic products, with an option for a third, leveraging Alteogen's ALT-B4 to convert IV infusions into more convenient SC forms.

feed.title: BlackRock cuts its Endeavour Mining position to 14.58%
feed.summary: BlackRock has reduced its total voting rights in Endeavour Mining (EDVMF) to 14.58% as of March 24, 2026, down from 14.89% previously. This new holding comprises 11.92% direct voting rights and 2.66% through financial instruments like securities lending and CFDs. The notification was filed by BlackRock's regulatory team on March 25, 2026.

feed.title: Global auto giant Ford builds first ever pickup truck with Nelson Mandela’s signature to support charity in South Africa
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company of Southern Africa has donated a unique Ford Ranger Wildtrak X, featuring Nelson Mandela’s signature, to the Nelson Mandela Children’s Fund (NMCF) for fundraising purposes. This special-edition vehicle symbolizes Ford's long-standing connection to South Africa and Mandela, whose first car was also a Ford. The proceeds from its sale will support the NMCF's community programs, highlighting Ford's commitment to social responsibility and child welfare in the region.

feed.title: The PNC Financial Services Group, Inc (NYSE:PNC) Given Average Rating of "Moderate Buy" by Brokerages

feed.title: U.S. and foreign governments order $60M in smallpox countermeasures
feed.summary: Emergent BioSolutions (NYSE: EBS) has secured over $60 million in new orders for smallpox medical countermeasures. This includes a $54 million contract option with the U.S. government for CNJ-016 (VIGIV) and $6.6 million in orders from an international government partner for ACAM2000. Additionally, the company referenced multi-year agreements with Canada valued up to $140 million CAD, with more than $35 million CAD expected in 2026.
